subject: A monstrous many-limbed worm-horror with reaching tendrils and clawed appendages bursts from the dark mouth of a stone culvert tunnel
description: Centered in the frame, a chitinous worm-horror erupts from a circular stone culvert opening, its body a tangle of segmented limbs, hooked claws, and thrashing tendrils that splay outward toward the viewer. The creature is rendered in sickly green and ochre tones, with darker shadowed crevices between its plated segments. The culvert's stonework arch frames the beast, with damp masonry and shadow receding into pitch-black depth behind it. Foreground detail emphasizes the predatory reach of the limbs, while the background dissolves into murky sewer gloom. The mood is ambush-horror — something wet, hungry, and many-legged lying in wait beneath the city.
foreground: many-limbed worm-horror creature lunging outward with claws and tendrils
background: stone culvert tunnel mouth receding into darkness
